title: Listening - Part 3
description: For each question, write the correct answer in the gap. Write one or two words or a number or a date or a time.
instructions: You will hear one person giving information. For questions 14–19, write the correct answer in the gap.
context: You will hear a teacher giving information about a school charity race.
audio_script: Speaker: Good afternoon, students. I'm here to tell you about our school charity race, which will take place next month. We are raising money for the children's hospital, and everyone can join in, even if you don't usually run. The race will be in [14]Riverside Park[/14], not on the school field, because there is more space there. Teachers will be at the park gates to show you where to go. The race begins at ten, but you must arrive by [15]nine thirty[/15] so we can give out numbers. Please wear sports clothes and bring a bottle of water. You should not bring your school bag, because there is nowhere safe to leave it. This year, each student will run [16]three[/16] kilometres. That sounds a lot, but you can walk some of the way if you need to. You can ask friends and family to sponsor you, but please do not ask strangers for money. If the weather is bad, we will still run, unless there is heavy rain or strong wind. Before the race, Mrs Green will lead a short [17]warm-up[/17] near the bandstand. It is important that everyone does this to avoid injuries.  There will be clear signs along the route, and older students will stand at corners to help. After the race, there will be sandwiches and fruit in the picnic area. Parents can come to watch, but they should use the [18]south gate[/18], because the main gate will be closed. The student who raises the most money will win a new [19]sports bag[/19]. Please collect your sponsor forms from your tutor tomorrow.

items.text_before: Before the race, students do a:

questions.explanation: The speaker says the race will be in Riverside Park.
Students must arrive by nine thirty.
Each student will run three kilometres.
